name: rad-planner
description:
Plan a project before you write code. rad-planner runs a rigorous planning conversation and produces one thing: a plan a fresh agent can execute. It is strictly a planner — it writes the plan and nothing else.

The method is risk-first, adversarially-reviewed, and mechanically-validated — more than single-pass planning:
- Surfaces non-obvious assumptions before sequencing anything
- Evaluates the stack for code-generation accuracy (AI-native Golden Path matrix), not just general fit
- Decomposes goal-backward (what must be TRUE / EXIST / be CRITICAL) and sequences risk-first with size discipline
- Specs every task to execution-readiness: objective, files, dependencies, done-when, validation, rollback
- Mechanically validates the plan with `plan-lint.py` (required sections, per-task fields, dependency resolution + cycles, vague-language detection) — a real pass/fail check, not the model grading itself
- Adversarially reviews via the `risk-assessor` agent against 14 documented anti-patterns, with APPROVE / REVISE / RETHINK verdicts and escalation to brainstorming when the architecture itself is broken

Output is a single file, `docs/plan.md` (created fresh or updated in place). Everything folds into it — objective, scope, assumptions, milestones, tasks, checkpoints, risks. No strategic-doc tree.

When the conversation surfaces something that belongs in a durable doc the planner does not own (a PRD / product contract, a decision log, an architecture reference), it does NOT write that doc. It writes a paste-ready `docs/[date]-update-prompt.md` you run in Claude or Codex to apply the change, and points `plan.md` at it — so durable docs stay under your control.

Two skills: `/plan` (the planning conversation) and `/review-plan` (a two-layer quality audit — mechanical lint + adversarial risk review — of an existing plan). Two agents back them: `stack-advisor` and `risk-assessor`.

**v5.0 — reboot.** Stripped the v4.x canonical doc tree, scope gates, depth heuristics, and doc-org validators that had buried the planning method. Restored the v3.0 conversation's rigor, kept the genuinely-good ideas (goal-backward decomposition, size discipline), and reshaped the output to a single lean plan. Decoupled from rad-session: no operating-manual or status-doc writes, no cross-plugin handoff file.
